title: Special handle --cpus for krun
body:
### Feature request description

krun, if no additional parameters are specified, assigns 16 vCPUs, while can usually be too much for a single threaded container and adds extra overhead. 

`--cpuset-cpus=` could limit the cores assigned, but it also binds the workload to specific cores and requires extra permission. 

`--cpus` just limits the time assigned but the krun instance will still use 16 vcpus.

### Suggest potential solution

Maybe the code change is related to krun, what I want is when `--cpus=2` is specified, the kvm instance will only have 2 cpus. 

### Have you considered any alternatives?

`--cpuset-cpus=`, at the cost of permission required + binding to specific cores.

### Additional context

This might be the job for krun, if so, feel free to move this issue.

comments.body: Hi @karuboniru, this is most likely an issue with how krun handles arguments from the container engine. Let me look into it a little more in the coming days to confirm. If my initial hunch is correct, I'll move it over to the crun repository and assign it to myself from there.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hi @karuboniru, apologies for the delay. Not long after you initially submitted this issue, I had merged an unrelated change in crun to allow the use of a `krun.cpus` annotation that would let you configure the vCPU count of the microVM (https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1950/changes/41941f2c28c1f72de1bf3fd1cd327b5f3d4ca526). Would that satisfy your use case? 

Something along the lines of `podman run --runtime krun --annotation "krun.cpus=2" alpine nproc` should output `2`.
Yes, thank you! This is exactly what I am looking for, closing the issue.
